codeReview.summary: The feature delivers a substantial graph-first shell with focus states, related-node traversal, deep-link restoration/fallback, and mobile/keyboard support, but it does not fully replace the remaining section-anchor browsing model.

issues.description: The hero's primary "See What's Live" CTA still links to `#proof` instead of a graph destination such as `#graph-proof`. That leaves a prominent legacy anchor-scroll path in place, so the feature does not fully satisfy the requirement to replace the remaining section-anchor browsing model with graph destinations and focus states.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `frontend-experience-worker` procedure should explicitly require verification of hero and CTA destinations when shell/navigation work claims to replace section-anchor browsing. This feature's tests cover `NAV_ITEMS` and `GraphShell` behavior, but they missed a remaining hero CTA that still targets `#proof`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `src/components/graph/__tests__/GraphShell.test.tsx` validates the shell in isolation and `src/app/page.tsx:101-113` still contains a primary CTA with `href="#proof"`.

summary: Fail: the graph shell itself is strong, but a prominent hero CTA still uses the legacy `#proof` section anchor instead of opening a graph focus state, so section-anchor browsing was not fully replaced.
